states.line: Dr. A. Cula, I assume Mr. Pig has explained the case as a financial weather event. Let us locate the law underneath it.
Find the procedural binder and the rights memo. One tells us the rule. The other reminds the court why the rule matters.
Good. Now we have a rule. We still need the rights point, unless we intend to argue fairness by facial expression.

idle_flavor: Nothing is presently on fire. In this office, that is not calm; it is a drafting window.
Evidence is not decoration. If it does not support an issue, it is paper with self-esteem.
Before court, ask three things: what happened, what rule matters, and what remedy we can honestly request.
